daily_brief: The conclusion of President Trump's two-day summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing dominated global headlines, with Taiwan emerging as a flashpoint after Trump warned the island against declaring formal independence [BBC], [France 24], [Nikkei Asia]. While both leaders described the talks as successful, few concrete trade deals were confirmed beyond a potential Boeing aircraft sale. In counterterrorism news, US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described by Trump as ISIS's global second-in-command, in a joint operation in the Lake Chad Basin [BBC], [France 24], [The Guardian]. A new Ebola outbreak in eastern DRC has killed 65 people, with the Africa CDC convening emergency talks as the Bundibugyo strain—for which no licensed vaccine exists—was identified [RFI], [The Guardian]. The Russia-Ukraine conflict saw a prisoner exchange of 205 POWs proceed even as Kyiv mourned 24 civilians killed in a Russian missile strike on an apartment block [BBC]. Meanwhile, Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their fragile ceasefire for 45 days despite ongoing Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon [France 24], [The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: Trump Warns Taiwan Against Independence After Concluding Summit with Xi in Beijing
bullets: Trump concluded a two-day state visit to Beijing, describing talks as 'very successful' while Xi called them 'historic and landmark'; Xi accepted an invitation to visit the White House in September
Trump told Fox News he is 'not looking to have somebody go independent' regarding Taiwan, though he said he made 'no commitment either way' on the self-governing island

Taiwan's Foreign Ministry responded Saturday by asserting it is a 'sovereign and independent democratic nation' not subordinate to China, and emphasized that US arms sales are part of Washington's security commitment
Few concrete trade deals were announced, though Trump claimed China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing jets with potential commitment for 750 more; the tariff truce expires in November

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized warm rhetoric and symbolism of visit, noted lack of confirmed deals

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on Taiwan's assertive response and sovereignty claims

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Taiwan's cautious relief and arms sales implications

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Taiwan's independence assertion and US security commitments

top_stories.headline: US and Nigerian Forces Kill Senior ISIS Leader in Joint Operation
bullets: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, designated by Trump as ISIS's global second-in-command, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in the Lake Chad Basin
Nigerian President Bola Tinubu confirmed al-Minuki was killed alongside 'several of his lieutenants' during a strike on his compound
Al-Minuki, a Nigerian national, directed IS-linked cells across Africa and was designated a Specially Designated Global Terrorist by Washington in 2023
Trump described the killing as significantly diminishing ISIS's global operations and African networks

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted this could mark significant setback for IS following al-Baghdadi's 2019 death

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Included detail that al-Minuki was plotting attacks against US interests

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Provided context on Trump's previous accusations against Nigeria regarding Christian persecution

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Brief factual coverage referencing earlier US strikes in Nigeria

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak in Eastern DRC Kills 65, Spreads to Uganda
bullets: Africa CDC reported 246 suspected Ebola cases and 65 deaths in DRC's conflict-affected Ituri province, near borders with Uganda and South Sudan
Uganda confirmed its first case after a 59-year-old man died in a Kampala hospital following travel from DRC
DRC health officials identified the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which no licensed vaccine exists, unlike the more common Zaire strain
Africa CDC convened emergency meeting with regional authorities, warning that 'intense population movement' in mining areas increases transmission risk

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized regional alert and cross-border concerns

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Highlighted that Bundibugyo strain has no available vaccine, noted only two previous outbreaks of this variant

top_stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine Prisoner Swap Proceeds as Kyiv Mourns 24 Killed in Apartment Strike
bullets: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war on Friday, with most Ukrainian POWs held since 2022
A Russian missile strike destroyed 18 flats in a Kyiv apartment block, killing 24 people including three girls; among victims was 12-year-old Lyubava Yakovleva, whose father died earlier in the war
The exchange occurred during a short-lived ceasefire that ended with massive Russian strikes across Ukraine
Russian officials reported four people killed, including a child, when Ukrainian drones struck Ryazan; Ukraine claimed it hit one of Russia's largest oil refineries

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of both the prisoner exchange and civilian casualties on both sides

top_stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire for 45 Days Despite Ongoing Strikes
bullets: The US announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their April 17 ceasefire by 45 days and hold expanded talks on a political settlement
At least six people were killed, including three paramedics, in an Israeli strike on a civil defence centre in southern Lebanon early Saturday
Israel maintains its strikes are not subject to the ceasefire terms; the original truce did not end fighting between Israel and Hezbollah
Iran's Foreign Minister said Tehran received messages from the US indicating willingness to continue talks

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US role in brokering extension and ongoing strikes

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iranian diplomatic openings and China's potential mediation role

additional_stories.headline: Switzerland announces it will open secret intelligence files on Nazi war criminal Josef Mengele
Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial ends in mistrial after jury deadlock
Man killed by 13-foot great white shark near Perth's Rottnest Island in Western Australia
Five Italian divers, including university researchers, die in cave diving accident in the Maldives
EU invites Taliban representatives to Brussels for technical talks on Afghan migrant deportations
Six million Somalis face crisis-level food insecurity as famine looms amid drought and Middle East war impacts
Spain's PM Sanchez defends Eurovision boycott over Israel's participation, citing Gaza genocide
Japan set to receive first LNG shipment via Strait of Hormuz since de facto closure due to Iran conflict
Indian PM Modi begins five-nation tour focused on trade and energy stability amid Gulf shipping disruptions
Mali's military junta launches airstrikes against rebel alliance that seized key northern town of Kidal
